rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FIORANI LIVING TRUST DTD. 1/25/02 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order: 1)Settling And Approving Receiver'S Final Accounting And Report; 2)Discharging Receiver; And 3) Exonerating Receiver'S Bond
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 18, 2021 line 8. NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER: 1) SETTLING AND APPROVING RECEIVER'S FINAL ACCOUNTING AND REPORT; 2)DISCHARGING RECEIVER; AND 3) EXONERATING RECEIVER'S BOND is continued to November 30, 2021. No later than November 22, 2021 David Bradlow to file a declaration clarifying the request for distribution to "conversatee, William Parisi." Bradlow October 21, 2021 Declaration 3:4. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CANTILLANO IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Petition
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Now before the Court is Petitioner Anthony Cantillano Sabada's Motion for Leave to File Amended Petition. The unopposed motion is GRANTED. The court may grant leave to amend the pleadings at any stage of the action. Motions for leave to amend the pleadings are directed to the sound discretion of the judge. "The court may, in furtherance of justice, and on any terms as may be proper, allow a party to amend any pleading." CCP Section 473(a)(1); see CCP Section 576. The court's discretion will usually be exercised liberally to permit amendment of the pleadings. See Nestle v. Santa Monica (1972) 6C.3d 920, 939; Mabie v. Hyatt (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 581, 596 (citing text); Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428. Courts are bound to apply a policy of great liberality in permitting amendments to the complaint "at any stage of the proceedings, up to and including trial," absent prejudice to the adverse party. Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761 (internal quotes omitted). Petitioner, Anthony Cantillano Sabada, shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. Petitioner is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative by the time set for this hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
